title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people describing their volunteering experiences and what they gained from them. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.A:
### Sophie, 22, Student
I started volunteering at a local animal shelter last year. [43]At first, I was nervous about handling animals, but the staff were supportive[/43]. Over time, I became more confident and [47]learned how to work as part of a team[/47]. The best part is seeing the animals find new homes. [50]I’ve also made friends with people I wouldn’t have met otherwise[/50], which has broadened my perspective.
texts.B:
### Marcus, 38, Accountant
I began volunteering to teach adults basic computer skills. [44]It was challenging to explain things simply, but seeing people gain confidence was rewarding[/44]. I’ve discovered that [48]helping others has made me more patient and understanding[/48]. [51]I now appreciate how small efforts can make a big difference in someone’s life[/51].
texts.C:
### Elena, 27, Nurse
I joined a project to support elderly people living alone. [45]Listening to their stories made me realise how important companionship is[/45]. I’ve learned a lot about resilience from them. [49]Sometimes, just being there to listen is the most valuable thing you can offer[/49]. [52]Volunteering has helped me develop empathy and gratitude[/52].
texts.D:
### Tom, 31, Graphic Designer
I volunteered to organise a community art event. [46]It was stressful at times, especially coordinating with so many people[/46], but seeing the event come together was worth it. [47]I discovered I enjoy bringing people together for a common cause[/47]. [49]The experience taught me to be more organised and flexible[/49].

questions.question: was initially unsure about their ability to do the volunteering work?
questions.explanation: Sophie was nervous about handling animals at first.

questions.question: found it difficult to teach in a simple way?
questions.explanation: Marcus found it challenging to explain things simply.

questions.question: realised the value of companionship through volunteering?
questions.explanation: Elena learned how important companionship is from the elderly.

questions.question: felt stressed by the responsibility of organising an event?
questions.explanation: Tom found coordinating the art event stressful at times.

questions.question: gained teamwork skills from their volunteering experience?
questions.explanation: Sophie learned to work as part of a team.

questions.question: became more patient and understanding as a result of volunteering?
questions.explanation: Marcus says helping others made him more patient and understanding.

questions.question: learned that simply being present can be valuable?
questions.explanation: Elena says just being there to listen is valuable.

questions.question: made new friends through volunteering?
questions.explanation: Sophie made friends she wouldn’t have met otherwise.

questions.question: came to appreciate the impact of small actions?
questions.explanation: Marcus appreciates how small efforts can make a big difference.

questions.question: feels they have become more empathetic and grateful?
questions.explanation: Elena says volunteering helped her develop empathy and gratitude.
